questions.question: Which of the following describes the most common hardware description language (HDL) used for designing digital circuits, enabling designers to model and simulate digital systems at various levels of abstraction?
options: Python
Java
Verilog
Assembly

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of a pipelined processor architecture in a CPU?
options: To reduce the overall power consumption of the processor
To decrease the clock frequency for more stable operation
To simplify the instruction set architecture (ISA)
To improve the instruction throughput by executing multiple instructions concurrently in different stages
To lower the latency of a single instruction execution

questions.question: In the context of power management for digital Integrated Circuits (ICs), what is the primary purpose of 'power gating'?
options: To dynamically adjust the clock frequency of active blocks based on workload
To increase the supply voltage to critical paths for higher performance
To completely shut off the power supply to inactive circuit blocks, primarily to eliminate leakage power
To isolate different voltage domains within an SoC to prevent voltage overshoots
To reduce dynamic power consumption during active switching by minimizing capacitance

questions.question: What is the primary architectural concept that allows a single processor core to execute multiple threads concurrently by rapidly switching between them or by executing instructions from different threads in parallel within the same execution pipeline, often used to hide memory latency?
options: Out-of-order execution
Pipelining
Superscalar execution
Simultaneous Multi-threading (e.g., Hyper-threading)
Vector processing

questions.question: In the typical digital IC design flow, what is the primary output generated by the 'Logic Synthesis' stage?
options: A behavioral C++ model describing the chip's functionality
The GDSII layout file ready for manufacturing
A netlist composed of standard cells (e.g., NAND, NOR, flip-flops) and their interconnections
A Verilog/VHDL Register-Transfer Level (RTL) description
A detailed physical floorplan of the chip
